name: Margilan
description: Margilan in Fergana Region is an ancient silk city of 2,000+ years and ~300,000 people, famous for the Yodgorlik silk factory, Said Ahmad Khoja madrasa and crafts. Continental climate; since 2007 jubilee of 2,000 years; known since 7th c. in sources. Atlas and adras ikat textiles are world-renowned.

monuments.title: Yodgorlik silk factory
monuments.info: Since 1924, major hand-woven natural silk workshop — from silkworm to fabric; visitors see the full process.

monuments.title: Said Ahmad Khoja Ishan Madrasa
monuments.info: c. 1907–09 by Sayyid Ahmadkhoja Ghiyas Khojayev; brick traditional complex, active mosque and shrine.

monuments.title: Pir Siddiq complex (Pigeon shrine)
monuments.info: Mid-18th c.; legend says pigeons saved the saint — thousands of birds still gather here.

monuments.title: Khoja Maus Mausoleum
monuments.info: First half 18th c. single-chamber memorial with refined decoration — quiet pilgrimage site.

monuments.title: Erkin Vahidov Creators’ Park
monuments.info: 2017 cultural park to poet and Hero of Uzbekistan Erkin Vahidov: statue, memorial museum and creative school.
